bullets.text: Q3 companywide CR 89.0 including 3.1 CR points from Hurricane Helene (Cat 4); Personal Lines CR 89.6; Commercial Lines CR 88.7, improving 10.4 points YOY — fifth consecutive quarter of double-digit improvement.
Q3 companywide NPW grew 25% YOY; Personal Lines NPW $16.3B (+29%); personal auto grew 25% driven by rate increases earning through and strong PIF growth from prior quarters.
Personal Lines PIFs reached 29.3 million, up 15% YOY; personal auto net new PIFs positive for the second consecutive quarter as Progressive opened more broadly to new business.

Hurricane Milton struck early Q4, generating estimated $140M property losses and $600M combined personal and commercial auto losses; Helene added 3.1 CR points in Q3; property benefited from ~30 points favorable prior-year development offsetting 7.5 points cat losses.
Personal property rates increased 6% in Q3 (20% on trailing four-quarter basis); personal auto rate increases largely complete — company pivoting focus from rate adequacy to growth.
Auto pricing model 8.9 live in 14 states (35% of PIFs); model 8.8 in 19 states; Snapshot telematics expansion continuing; property 5.0+ models in 19 states covering a majority of homeowners NPW.
Debt-to-total capital at 20.2% at Q3 end; investment portfolio returned 4.0% YTD; fixed income benefiting from higher reinvestment rates as portfolio rolls into new bonds.
Commercial Lines BOP and specialty products expanding; for-hire transportation and contractor segments growing; property Blueprint strategy advancing targeted growth in non-catastrophe-prone states.
Personal auto market share gains accelerating as competitors remained restrictive; Progressive's early return to target profitability created a 6–12 month competitive advantage in growth positioning.

Personal auto industry broadly achieving rate adequacy by Q3 2024; competitors beginning to re-open for business; Progressive expects competitive environment to intensify but maintains pricing discipline.
